name: SPAGHETTI BOLOGNESE
description: Could this be the world's favourite comfort meal? Our spag bol has all the deliciousness of the original, but with minced mushrooms and walnuts replacing the meat. It's surprisingly satisfying and warming. Perfect for a date night and great comfort food for a warming, satisfying and healthy(ish) dinner, then look no further. Perfect for a date night and great eaten with red wine.
ingredients: handful fresh basil leaves, to serve
method: Food processor | Large frying pan on a high heat | Large saucepan | Kettle boiled
Put the mushrooms in the food processor and pulse until you have minced (you can stop chopping it by hand once it's evenly processed, processor).

Pour the olive oil into the frying pan. Add the mushrooms and season with a small pinch of salt and pepper. Cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring regularly, until all the liquid has evaporated and they're lovely and golden. Transfer the mushrooms to a bowl and set aside.

Peel and roughly chop the carrots. Peel the garlic. Trim the top and bottom ends of the celery stalks and roughly chop. Peel the celery, carrots and peel the swish in a bowl and roughly chop them. Put all the vegetables and garlic into the food processor and pulse until finely chopped.

Put the same pan back on a medium-high heat and add oil. Add the minced onions, carrots, celery and garlic and cook for about 10 minutes until all the vegetables are soft. Stir in the tomato paste. Add the wine, balsamic vinegar, oregano, bay leaf and mushrooms. Bring to the boil, then turn down the heat. Simmer for 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, put the large saucepan on a high heat and fill it to two-thirds with a big pinch of salt and bring to the boil. Add the pasta to the pan and cook until al dente, following the instructions on the packet. Drain and tip the water into a cup and set aside. Drain the pasta.

Taste the sauce and season with salt and pepper. Add the pasta and reserved pasta water. Stir everything together, adding a little more pasta water if needed. Turn off the heat and pour in the reserved pasta water through.
Pour the sauce into the pasta pot and stir everything together so that the sauce completely covers the pasta. Scatter over the basil leaves and grind over some black pepper. Serve to happy faces!
